questions.q: What is the primary function of a service industry in a secondary activity?
opts: (A) Production and distribution of goods
(B) Provision of services to meet human needs
(C) Processing of raw materials
(D) Extraction of natural resources
questions.explanation: Service industries provide services to meet human needs, such as transportation, communication, and tourism.

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of a secondary activity?
opts: (A) Agriculture
(B) Manufacturing
(C) Service industry
(D) Primary activity
questions.explanation: Manufacturing is an example of a secondary activity because it involves the processing of raw materials to produce goods.

questions.q: What is the difference between a primary and secondary activity in terms of the type of goods produced?
opts: (A) Primary activities produce raw materials, while secondary activities produce finished goods
(B) Primary activities produce finished goods, while secondary activities produce raw materials
(C) Primary activities produce goods for local consumption, while secondary activities produce goods for export
(D) Primary activities produce goods for export, while secondary activities produce goods for local consumption
questions.explanation: Primary activities produce raw materials, while secondary activities produce finished goods.
questions.topic: Comparison of Primary and Secondary Activities

questions.q: Which of the following is a characteristic of a secondary activity?
opts: (A) It is seasonal and labor-intensive
(B) It involves the processing of raw materials to produce goods
(C) It is a primary activity
(D) It involves the extraction of natural resources
questions.explanation: Secondary activities involve the processing of raw materials to produce goods.

questions.q: What is the role of transportation in a secondary activity?
opts: (A) To provide services to meet human needs
(B) To process raw materials to produce goods
(C) To facilitate the movement of goods and people
(D) To extract natural resources
questions.explanation: Transportation plays a crucial role in facilitating the movement of goods and people in a secondary activity.
questions.topic: Role of Transportation in Secondary Activities

questions.q: Which of the following is a type of secondary activity?
opts: (A) Service industry
(B) Primary activity
(C) Manufacturing
(D) Tourism
questions.explanation: Manufacturing is a type of secondary activity because it involves the processing of raw materials to produce goods.

questions.q: What is the impact of a secondary activity on the environment?
opts: (A) It has a positive impact on the environment
(B) It has a negative impact on the environment
(C) It has no impact on the environment
(D) It depends on the type of secondary activity
questions.explanation: Secondary activities can have a negative impact on the environment due to pollution, deforestation, and other factors.

questions.q: Which of the following is a benefit of a secondary activity?
opts: (A) Increased employment opportunities
(B) Decreased employment opportunities
(C) Increased poverty
(D) Decreased standard of living
questions.explanation: Secondary activities can create employment opportunities and increase economic growth.

questions.q: What is the relationship between a secondary activity and the tertiary sector?
opts: (A) They are the same sector
(B) The tertiary sector is a subset of the secondary sector
(C) The secondary sector is a subset of the tertiary sector
(D) They are unrelated
questions.explanation: The secondary sector is a subset of the tertiary sector, which includes service industries and other activities.
questions.topic: Relationship between Secondary and Tertiary Sectors

questions.q: Which of the following is a characteristic of a secondary activity in terms of its location?
opts: (A) It is located in a rural area
(B) It is located in an urban area
(C) It is not location-dependent
(D) It is located near a natural resource
questions.explanation: Secondary activities are often located in urban areas due to the availability of infrastructure and labor.

questions.q: What is the impact of a secondary activity on the local economy?
opts: (A) It has a positive impact on the local economy
(B) It has a negative impact on the local economy
(C) It has no impact on the local economy
(D) It depends on the type of secondary activity
questions.explanation: Secondary activities can stimulate economic growth and create employment opportunities in the local economy.
questions.topic: Impact of Secondary Activities on Local Economy

questions.q: Which of the following is a type of secondary activity in terms of its technology?
opts: (A) Low-tech industry
(B) High-tech industry
(C) Traditional industry
(D) Modern industry
questions.explanation: High-tech industries, such as electronics and software, are examples of secondary activities that utilize advanced technology.
questions.topic: Types of Secondary Activities based on Technology

questions.q: What is the relationship between a secondary activity and the primary sector?
opts: (A) They are the same sector
(B) The primary sector is a subset of the secondary sector
(C) The secondary sector is a subset of the primary sector
(D) They are unrelated
questions.explanation: The primary sector is a subset of the secondary sector, which includes activities such as manufacturing and construction.
questions.topic: Relationship between Secondary and Primary Sectors

questions.q: Which of the following is a characteristic of a secondary activity in terms of its scale of operation?
opts: (A) It is a small-scale industry
(B) It is a large-scale industry
(C) It is not scale-dependent
(D) It is a cottage industry
questions.explanation: Secondary activities can be large-scale industries that produce goods in mass quantities.
questions.topic: Scale of Operation of Secondary Activities

questions.q: What is the impact of a secondary activity on the social structure of a community?
opts: (A) It has a positive impact on the social structure
(B) It has a negative impact on the social structure
(C) It has no impact on the social structure
(D) It depends on the type of secondary activity
questions.explanation: Secondary activities can lead to social changes, such as urbanization and the movement of people from rural to urban areas.
questions.topic: Impact of Secondary Activities on Social Structure

questions.q: Which of the following is a benefit of a secondary activity in terms of its contribution to the GDP?
opts: (A) It increases the GDP
(B) It decreases the GDP
(C) It has no impact on the GDP
(D) It depends on the type of secondary activity
questions.explanation: Secondary activities can contribute significantly to the GDP by increasing economic growth and production.
questions.topic: Benefits of Secondary Activities in terms of GDP

questions.q: What is the relationship between a secondary activity and the tertiary sector in terms of employment opportunities?
opts: (A) They have the same level of employment opportunities
(B) The tertiary sector has more employment opportunities
(C) The secondary sector has more employment opportunities
(D) They have no relationship
questions.explanation: The secondary sector often has more employment opportunities than the tertiary sector due to the nature of its activities.
questions.topic: Relationship between Secondary and Tertiary Sectors in terms of Employment Opportunities
